description: In the ACT, learner and provisional drivers must maintain zero BAC while driving.
In wet weather, reduce speed and increase following distance because stopping distances increase and visibility may be reduced.
You must slow to 40 km/h when passing stationary emergency vehicles displaying flashing red or blue lights.
Do not drive if fatigue, illness, alcohol, drugs or medication could impair your ability to drive safely.
Scanning ahead and checking mirrors regularly helps drivers identify hazards early and react safely.
Allow extra space around heavy vehicles and avoid staying in blind spots when sharing the road.
Vehicles and trailers must not exceed legal towing or load limits and loads must be secured properly.
A NO U-TURN sign means making a U-turn at that location is prohibited.
You must not park too close to intersections, traffic lights or pedestrian crossings unless signs permit it.
Drivers must give way to pedestrians at crossings and when turning into a road pedestrians are crossing.
Police may conduct random breath testing, and drivers are required to provide a breath sample when directed.
School zones in the ACT generally operate at 40 km/h during the times shown on school zone signs.
At a stop sign or stop line, you must stop completely and give way before proceeding.
Increase your following distance in wet conditions to allow extra time and distance for braking.
Avoid following trucks too closely because visibility and stopping distance are reduced.
Obey temporary speed limits, signs and traffic controllers when driving through roadwork areas.
Bus lanes may only be used by permitted vehicles unless signs indicate otherwise.
Loading zones are reserved for authorised vehicles and misuse may result in fines or penalties.
